extraction.one_line_summary: KRISTOFF Alexander won the sprint after VAN AVERMAET Greg and BARDET Romain's late attack was caught inside 2km.
tactical_patterns: BARDET attacking with VAN AVERMAET despite both lacking sprint finish suggests desperation to avoid Kristoff's speed
KEISSE Iljo escaped early on climb of Al Hamri rather than flat terrain, revealing crosswind sections were neutralized
HERMANS Ben crashes near climb base yet still secures GC, exposing weak final stage offensive efforts by rivals

rider_signals.KRISTOFF Alexander: positive: third stage win demonstrates unmatched sprint form when positioned correctly through chaotic finale
rider_signals.VAN AVERMAET Greg: neutral: willing to attack with Bardet inside 2km despite minimal winning chance reveals opportunistic racing over calculated risk
rider_signals.BARDET Romain: neutral: initiating late attack on stage with multiple crosswind sections suggests team failed earlier to create selection
rider_signals.HERMANS Ben: positive: surviving early crash near climb then defending GC without team support shows individual resilience under pressure
rider_signals.KEISSE Iljo: neutral: leading breakaway on climb rather than forcing crosswind selection indicates questionable tactical choice from experienced classics rider
